Metadata.title: Astra OS — AI Operating System | Join the Closed Beta
Metadata.description: Linux-based AI OS with kernel-level agent orchestration. First 100 developers shape the platform.

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.intro: Astra OS collects only the data you voluntarily provide during waitlist signup. We do not sell, rent, or share your data with third parties.
Privacy.dataCollectedItems:
First name, last name, and email address (required)
Answers to 4 qualifying questions (role, AI agent usage, interests, motivation)
Browser locale and referral source (analytics only, not linked to your identity)
Privacy.retentionBody: Your data is retained for the duration of the closed-beta period. You may request deletion at any time by emailing the address below.
Privacy.rightsBody: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data. For KVKK rights (Turkish residents), the same rights apply under Turkish data protection law.
Privacy.contactBody: For any privacy-related requests, email: privacy@astraos.com

Hero.headline: Your OS doesn't know what an AI agent is. Astra does.
Hero.subheadline: Astra OS is a Linux-based operating system with kernel-level AI agent orchestration — built for developers who are serious about what comes next.
Hero.scarcity: Closed beta. Limited to the first 100.

Features.sectionTitle: Built differently, from the ground up.
Features.card1Body: Built on a hardened Linux kernel — not a wrapper, not a compatibility layer. The same proven foundation that runs the world's most critical infrastructure, now optimized for AI workloads.
Features.card2Body: Every line of Astra OS that can be open will be. No black boxes, no lock-in. You can inspect the scheduler, audit the agent runtime, and fork whatever you need.
Features.card3Body: AI agent scheduling happens at the kernel, not in userspace middleware. Agents get real process isolation, direct hardware access, and first-class resource guarantees — not best-effort async glue.

WhyAstra.para1: macOS is built for humans using applications. The kernel scheduler does not know what a language model is, and Apple's security model treats AI agents as just another process — one it can silently throttle, sandbox, or kill. The result: agents lose context, pipelines break, and you spend your afternoon diagnosing permission errors instead of shipping.
WhyAstra.para2: Ubuntu plus whatever AI framework is popular this month gets you further — but you are still duct-taping a general-purpose OS to a problem it was never designed for. Agent orchestration lives in Python libraries. Process isolation is an afterthought. Every agent run competes with your desktop environment for the same unmanaged thread pool.
WhyAstra.para3: Astra OS starts from a different question: what if the OS knew agents were the primary workload? Kernel-level scheduling priorities for agent processes. First-class inter-agent communication via IPC primitives, not HTTP. Resource quotas enforced at the scheduler, not the application layer. This is not a framework — it is the operating system itself.

FinalCTA.headline: Ready to shape the future of AI infrastructure?
FinalCTA.scarcity: Seats fill fast. We're reviewing applications now.
